meta.notes: Based on Jan's onboarding doc. Update 'done' field as items complete.

phases.summary: Full immersion — understand the product, team, community, competitive landscape. Land a quick win.

items.task: Set up WebStorm as primary IDE
Join #advocates-only Slack
Join #ask-the-advocates Slack
Join #advocacy-coffee Slack
Join #webstorm-internal Slack
Join #webstorm-duties Slack
Join #webstorm-product Slack
Get YouTrack access — bookmark WebStorm board
Set up Hector standup command: /hector standup adv add [update] [ticket]
Log into HiBob (HR platform — PTO, sick leave, goals)
Read the Advocate's Manifesto
Read WebStorm roadmap and strategy docs
Meet Helen Scott (Head of Advocacy)
Meet Vlad Minaev (WebStorm Team Lead)
Meet Craig Walker (WebStorm Product Manager)
Meet Kristina Pchelintseva (WebStorm Product Manager)
Meet Web Head of Ecosystem (TBD — find out who this is)
Meet WebStorm Product Marketing Manager (TBD)
Attend demo, stand-up (if timezone allows), and team calls
Review internal docs: positioning, past campaigns, strategy
Browse open YouTrack tickets — get a feel for community frustrations
Start planning a piece of content — aim for a quick win
Audit WebStorm content channels: blog, YouTube, social
Deep dive into Reddit, Discord servers, Twitter/X, BlueSky, LinkedIn
Identify 20 most influential WebStorm community members (check with Marketing for baseline)
Competitive review: what Cursor, VS Code, and Zed advocates are publishing
Publish first piece of content — visible win internally and externally
items.notes: Amsterdam livestream Mar 25 will be this

items.task: State of the WebStorm Community memo
items.notes: v0.2 memo drafted — needs final polish

items.task: Competitive Content Gap Analysis
Community Champion Longlist (20 devs mapped by platform, audience, alignment)

phases.summary: Find your role in the community and build the product bridge.

items.task: First video tutorial produced and published to YouTube
Draft content calendar for next 60 days with 3 content pillars defined
Active daily presence on Reddit, Dev.to, Twitter/X
Initiate relationships with first 5 JBCCs (JetBrains Community Contributors)
Submit first conference talk (React Conf, VueConf, or framework-specific — in agreement with WebStorm Team / Head of Ecosystem)
Deliver first community insights report to product team (3 pain points with evidence)
Establish weekly sync with product managers
Identify 3 WebStorm improvements developers would talk about if they knew they were coming
Published video content pieces
First conference talk abstract submitted
Community Engagement Framework (documented playbook for how JetBrains/WebStorm engages in community spaces)

phases.summary: Content becomes a series, community relationships compound, strategy gets formalized.

items.task: Plan and start 3-part content series (e.g. 'WebStorm Mastery' or 'AI + WebStorm for Real Teams')
Ship at least one piece that earns external links, shares, or community discussion
Establish weekly or bi-weekly publishing cadence for rest of year
Grow WebStorm community presence (JBCC / Discord / Reddit)
Propose clear OKRs for rest of year: content output, community growth, feedback volume
Synthesize 90 days of community data into strategic roadmap recommendations for product team
Present 'State of WebStorm Advocacy' — what's working, what's not, what's next
Content series concept (3+ pieces) with engagement metrics and repeatable production workflow
5+ JBCCs onboarded
OKRs + Annual Advocacy Plan
